parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets REF and DIV rising-edge threshold

parameters.name: tedge
parameters.override_behavior: sets UP and DN edge smoothing

modeling_constraints: Use deterministic voltage-domain rising-edge behavior for REF and DIV.
Implement mutual-clear behavior internally; do not add an external reset port.
Drive UP and DN with voltage contributions and finite smoothing referenced to local VDD/VSS.
Do not use transistor-level devices, current contributions, ddt(), idt(), validation hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.

properties.observable_contract: A rising REF edge asserts UP, and falling REF edges do not set either output.
A rising DIV edge asserts DN, and falling DIV edges do not set either output.
If a rising edge arrives while the opposite output state is already high, both UP and DN clear immediately for REF-leading and DIV-leading orderings.
UP and DN are not intentionally held high together beyond finite transition smoothing overlap.
UP and DN high levels track the local VDD rail and low levels track the local VSS rail.

title: PFD Up DN Logic
